# Maintainability Checklist: GitHub Pages Build-And-Deploy Pipeline

**Purpose**: Validate maintainability requirements for a small standard Pages workflow, no custom deploy helper, reviewable file scope, DOC-012-removable indexing guard, and concise CLAUDE.md guidance before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-23
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Simplicity And Standard Deployment

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements explicit that DOC-011 uses standard GitHub Pages Actions unless a stated requirement cannot be met that way? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-018]
- [x] CHK002 Is the no-custom-helper boundary specific enough to prevent adding a deploy script, API wrapper, or CLI setup helper without documented justification? [Clarity, Spec §FR-018, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [x] CHK003 Are workflow requirements limited to the existing docs-site install, validation, artifact upload, and Pages deploy path rather than adding new deployment abstractions? [Consistency, Spec §Pages Validation And Artifact Setup, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
- [x] CHK004 Is the manual Pages setup boundary consistent with the KISS/YAGNI principle against speculative automation? [Consistency, Spec §FR-016, Constitution §VI]

## Reviewable Scope Control

- [x] CHK005 Are final file operations bounded to deploy workflow setup, workflow validation, staging crawler guard, release PR docs-reference runtime alignment, one runbook, shared spec-index guard remediation, and concise CLAUDE.md guidance? [Completeness, Spec §Reviewability Notes]
- [x] CHK006 Does the spec define non-goals that keep plugin runtime behavior, release publication semantics, repository Pages settings automation, custom-domain launch, SEO launch work, analytics, and Lighthouse CI outside DOC-011? [Coverage, Spec §Reviewability Notes]
- [x] CHK007 Is the reviewability budget measurable enough for reviewers to detect path-filter or runbook scope creep before implementation expands? [Measurability, Spec §Reviewability Budget, Plan §Reviewability Budget]
- [x] CHK008 Are the roadmap, spec, and plan aligned that the slice stays one bounded staging-deploy capability rather than a broader production-launch effort? [Consistency, Roadmap §DOC-011, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Split decision]

## DOC-012 Indexing Guard Handoff

- [x] CHK009 Is the noindex/nofollow guard specified as a single Starlight `head` entry so DOC-012 can remove one obvious configuration block? [Clarity, Spec §Staging Visibility And Operator Documentation]
- [x] CHK010 Is `robots.txt` specified as an exact two-line file so DOC-012 can replace or remove the staging crawler policy without interpreting custom rules? [Clarity, Spec §Staging Visibility And Operator Documentation]
- [x] CHK011 Do the noindex meta entry, robots policy, runbook, and PR packet requirements all name DOC-012 as the launch/removal owner? [Traceability, Spec §FR-013, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements]
- [x] CHK012 Are staging protection requirements clear that previewability remains intact while public indexing and crawler discovery stay deferred? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Assumptions]

## Runbook And Agent Guidance Maintainability

- [x] CHK013 Are runbook requirements assigned to `docs/ai/specs/cicd-release-pipeline-verification.md` rather than duplicated as long-form operational instructions in CLAUDE.md? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK014 Is CLAUDE.md guidance required only to summarize the deploy workflow and point to the runbook, keeping future agent guidance short and source-linked? [Clarity, Spec §Staging Visibility And Operator Documentation]
- [x] CHK015 Are setup, retry, rollback, deployment-history, and DOC-012 handoff details required in the runbook where maintainers expect operational procedures? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §Staging Visibility And Operator Documentation]
- [x] CHK016 Can PR reviewers objectively compare CLAUDE.md against the runbook requirement to reject duplicated long-form runbook content? [Measurability, Spec §FR-015]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: small standard workflow, no unjustified custom deployment helper, bounded file operations, easy DOC-012 removal of indexing protection, and avoiding long-form runbook duplication in CLAUDE.md.
- Depth level: Standard maintainability requirements quality review.
- Actor/timing: Reviewer before DOC-011 implementation tasks.

## Verification Re-run

- [x] CHK017 Are standard Pages Actions, fixed permissions, separate build/deploy jobs, and same-run artifact requirements still sufficient without a custom deployment helper? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-018, Contract §Required Jobs]
- [x] CHK018 Are declared file operations and non-goals still bounded enough to keep the workflow, guard, runbook, and agent guidance reviewable as one DOC-011 slice? [Reviewability, Plan §Declared File Operations, Spec §Reviewability Notes]
- [x] CHK019 Are the Starlight head entry, exact robots policy, and DOC-012 ownership still written so the staging indexing guard has a clear removal path? [Clarity, Spec §Staging Visibility And Operator Documentation]
- [x] CHK020 Are full operational details still assigned to the CI/CD verification runbook while CLAUDE.md remains a concise pointer and summary? [Maintainability, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015]
